analysis.summary: Spa Tre, a 60-year-old female with no significant medical history or current medications, reports neck pain for 4-5 days with a severity of 5/10.

flags.title: Incomplete Symptom Information
flags.description: Crucial details regarding the nature of the neck pain (e.g., onset, aggravating/alleviating factors, radiation, associated neurological symptoms, or trauma history) are missing, which are essential for proper assessment and diagnosis.

possible_conditions.name: Musculoskeletal Strain
possible_conditions.explanation: General neck pain without other specific symptoms often points to muscle strain from poor posture, overuse, or minor injury.

possible_conditions.name: Cervical Spondylosis
possible_conditions.explanation: Degenerative changes in the cervical spine are common at age 60 and can cause chronic neck pain, stiffness, and potentially radicular symptoms if nerve roots are involved.

possible_conditions.name: Tension Headache
possible_conditions.explanation: Neck tension can often lead to tension-type headaches, which can present as pain in the neck and head.

recommendations: Apply heat or cold packs to the affected area for temporary relief.
Consider over-the-counter pain relievers like ibuprofen or acetaminophen if there are no contraindications.
Maintain good posture and avoid activities that aggravate the pain.
Consult with a doctor for a comprehensive evaluation to determine the underlying cause of the neck pain and receive appropriate treatment.
questions_for_doctor: Can you describe the exact nature of the pain (e.g., sharp, dull, aching, throbbing, constant, intermittent)?
Does the pain radiate to your shoulders, arms, hands, or head?
Do you experience any numbness, tingling, weakness, or other neurological symptoms in your arms or hands?
Did the pain start suddenly, gradually, or after any specific incident or injury?
What makes the pain better or worse (e.g., movement, rest, specific positions, medications)?
Do you have any associated symptoms such as stiffness, fever, fatigue, or difficulty with daily activities due to the pain?
